english: dpyid-ka (dpyid ka) šar (shar) spring has appeared; frq. of thoughts: nyáms-su (nyams su), or yid-la (yid la) ˳čár-ba ('char ba) (thoughts) rising in one s mind; yid-la (yid la) šar (shar) kyaṅ (kyang) Mil. though I can figure it in my mind; grógs-su (grogs su) ˳čar ('char) (they) appear as friends Mil.; rgyán-du (rgyan du) ˳čar ('char) Mil. it turned into a blessing. — ˳čár-sgo ('char sgo) thought, idea, conception, ˳čár-sgo ('char sgo) byuṅ (byung) an idea comes, a (happy) thought, a (new) light, bursts upon me Mil.; ˳čar-ga ('char ga) Mil. the rising, the rise.

secondary form to ˳čól-ba ('chol ba) II., 1. Cs.: to fluctuate mentally; in this sense prob. Zam. ytad-méd (gtad med) ˳čál-ba ('chal ba) to fluctuate, to waver, without aim or object. — 2. to be confused, in disorder, smra-˳čál (smra 'chal), also ˳čal-ytám ('chal gtam) smra (smra) Lt., as a morbid symptom, prob. he raves, he talks nonsense. — 3. morally: tsul-khrims (tshul khrims) ˳čál-ba ('chal ba) S.g. to break one's vow, bsláb-pa (bslab pa) to act contrary to the doctrine, to violate it Tar.; in a more restricted sense: — 4. to fornicate, to commit adultery, bud-méd (bud med) smad-˳čál (smad 'chal) byéd-pa (byed pa) a whore, harlot Mil.; ˳čál-pa ('chal pa), -po (-po) lecher, fornicator Stg.; ˳čál-pa-rnams-kyi ('chal pa rnams kyi) tsig (tshig) obscene language, mentioned as sub-species of kyál-ka (kyal ka); ˳čál-mo ('chal mo) whore. — *čal-la-čol-le* (*chal la chol le*) W., čal-čol (chal chol) Tar. 184, 20 confusedly, pellmell.

pf. ší (shi), 1. vb. to die, of a flame: to go out; raṅ (rang) ˳čio ('chi'o) I will seek death Dzl.; ˳čí-ba ('chi ba) yin (yin) he dies, will die S.g.; ˳či ('chi) or ši-ba-las (shi ba las) sós-par (sos par) gyúr-ba (gyur ba) Dzl. to be saved from imminent danger of death (but not: to rise from the dead); ˳čí-bar ('chi bar) byéd-pai (byed pa'i) ču (chu) water causing death Sambh.; ši-bar (shi bar) gyúr-to (gyur to) they perished Pth. — 2. sbst., the state of dying, death, ˳čí-ba ('chi ba) tsám-du (tsam du) gyúr-ba (gyur ba) to die almost (of grief etc.) Mil.; dus-mín (dus min) ˳čí-ba ('chi ba) nyuṅ-ba (nyung ba) yin (yin) premature death rarely occurs Sambh.; ˳čí-ba ('chi ba) nam (nam) yoṅ (yong) ča (cha) med (med) Mil. when death will come one does not know, (W. *ši-če* (*shi che*) to die; death; *ši soṅ* (*shi song*) he has died, *ši yin* (*shi yin*) he will die). Comp. ˳čí-ka ('chi ka) Cs.: 'the very act of dying,' but I doubt whether such a sbst. exists; I only know the adv. ˳čí-kar ('chi kar) at his very dying, at the point of death Mil., when being exstinguished Glr. (v. kar (kar) sub ka (ka) IV. 4, 5), and ˳čí-ka-ma ('chi ka ma) 1. adj. dying, dúd-gro (dud gro) ˳čí-ka-ma ('chi ka ma) a dying animal Glr.; 2. sbst. the dying, ˳čí-ka-ma-ru ('chi ka ma ru) = ˳čí-kar ('chi kar) (doubtful); ˳čí-kar ('chi kar) and ˳čí-gar ('chi gar) may be incorrect spellings. — ˳čí-ltas ('chi ltas), more rarely ˳či(-bai) ('chi( ba'i)) rtágs (rtags) forebodings, foretokens of death Med. — ˳čí-bdág ('chi bdag) the lord of death, perh. = yšín-rje (gshin rje), but it seems to be more a poetical expression than a mythological personage; ˳čí-bdág ('chi bdag) bdud (bdud) id. — ˳čí-nád ('chi nad) a disease causing death, a fatal disease Tar. — ˳čí-ba-po ('chi ba po) Cs.; a person dying (?) — ˳čí-(ba)-méd(-pa) ('chi (ba) med (pa)) immortal; cf. ši-ba (shi ba). — Note. ˳či ('chi) pó-ba ('pho ba) is prob. only a rather incorrect, yet common expression for tse (tshe) pó-ba ('pho ba) to change one's place of existence, to transmigrate.
to bind Sch., prob. an incorr. spelling for kyíg-pa (khyig pa).

I. vb., pf. bcíṅs (bcings), fut. bcíṅ (bcing), imp. ˳číṅ(s) ('ching(s)), W. *čiṅ-če* (*ching che*), to bind (in general); to fetter (a prisoner) Dzl.; to bind or tie up, to cord, a bundle or package; to tie round, to put on, a girdle Glr.; to bind up, to dress, wounds; fig. to render harmless, to neutralize, paralyze, esp. by witchcraft, to exorcise, frq.; bcíṅs (bcings) gról-ba (grol ba) to untie, to loosen, to take off the dressings Lt. — II. sbst. any binding-material 1. ribbon, mgul-číṅs (mgul chings) necklace, neckcloth; neckerchief. — 2. fetter, shackle, also fig. for magic curse, anathema. — 3. string, tie. — 4. cramp, spasm C.
a spurious, glass jewel (Schf. Tar. 142, 9); bsam-yas-˳číṅ-bu (bsam yas 'ching bu) p. n. Ma.
pf. bcíbs (bcibs), fut. bcíb (bcib), imp. číbs (chibs) resp. to ascend, to mount, a horse or carriage, rtá-la (rta la), or more correctly číbs-la (chibs la), to ride, to proceed on horseback.
to be full, to get full Sch.
